phase1.explanation:
Interfaces define a contract that classes must follow:

Key features:
• 100% abstract (pre-Java 8) — methods have no body
• All methods are public abstract by default
• All fields are public static final by default
• A class can implement MULTIPLE interfaces

Default methods (Java 8+):
• Provide default implementation
• Can be overridden
• Maintains backward compatibility

Static methods (Java 8+):
• Belong to the interface, not classes
• Called via InterfaceName.method()

Interface vs Abstract Class:
• Interface: multiple inheritance, no state (fields)
• Abstract class: single inheritance, can have state

When to use interfaces:
• Define capabilities (what a class can do)
• Multiple inheritance of type
• Unrelated classes share behavior

phase2.errorMessage:
Circle.java:10: error: draw() in Circle cannot implement draw() in Drawable
    void draw() {
         ^
  attempting to assign weaker access privileges; was public
1 error
phase2.explanation:
Interface methods are implicitly public. When implementing, you must use public!

The error says you're trying to assign weaker access (package-private) than the interface requires (public).

FIX: public void draw() { ... }

Always use public when implementing interface methods!

phase3.prompt:
Create an interface Playable with:
• void play()
• default void stop() — prints "Stopping playback"

Create MusicPlayer and VideoPlayer classes that implement Playable:
• MusicPlayer: play() prints "Playing music 🎵"
• VideoPlayer: play() prints "Playing video 🎬"

In main, create both and call play() and stop() on each.
dialogueHints: Playable interface needs play() and a default stop()
stop() is already provided by default — can use as-is
phase3.openingDialogue: Interfaces are contracts — sign them and deliver! 📋
